questions.text: When a project you worked on fails, what do you typically do?
options: A. Identify what I could have done better and share learnings
B. Analyze both team and external factors objectively
C. Focus on external reasons that caused the failure
D. Wait for others to raise the issue

questions.text: You are assigned a task with unclear instructions. What’s your approach?
options: A. Seek clarification and proceed independently
B. Try to complete it based on assumptions
C. Delay until someone provides detailed guidance
D. Ask for step-by-step instructions for everything

questions.text: A deadline is at risk due to your delay. What do you do?
options: A. Inform stakeholders early and propose solutions
B. Try to fix it quietly without informing anyone
C. Blame dependencies or other team members
D. Wait until asked

questions.text: How do you handle disagreements with another team?
options: A. Listen actively and find common ground
B. Stick to my approach since I know it works
C. Escalate immediately
D. Avoid interaction

questions.text: A teammate suggests a different approach than yours. What do you do?
options: A. Evaluate their idea objectively
B. Reject it if it differs from mine
C. Ignore their input
D. Accept it without discussion

questions.text: How do you typically work in team projects?
options: A. Collaboratively with regular communication
B. Independently with minimal interaction
C. Only when asked
D. Prefer working alone

questions.text: How do you respond to constructive criticism?
options: A. Reflect and improve
B. Accept but don’t act on it
C. Feel defensive
D. Dismiss it

questions.text: When under stress, how do you behave?
options: A. Stay calm and prioritize tasks
B. Show frustration occasionally
C. Get easily overwhelmed and react emotionally
D. Withdraw completely

questions.text: If a colleague is upset, what would you do?
options: A. Listen and support them
B. Offer advice immediately
C. Ignore it—it’s not my concern
D. Tell them to focus on work

questions.text: When facing a challenging task outside your expertise:
options: A. Take it as a learning opportunity
B. Try but avoid deep involvement
C. Decline if uncomfortable
D. Wait for someone else
A. Use it to improve continuously
B. Accept only if I agree
C. Avoid feedback
D. Feel discouraged

questions.text: When you fail at something new:
options: A. Learn and try again
B. Try once more cautiously
C. Avoid similar situations
D. Give up

questions.text: How do you influence others without authority?
options: A. Through clear communication and trust
B. By insisting on my ideas
C. Avoid influencing
D. Escalate to managers
A. Step up and help define priorities
B. Wait for leadership instructions
C. Focus only on my tasks
D. Complain about lack of clarity

questions.text: How do you motivate team members?
options: A. Encourage, support, and align goals
B. Focus only on results
C. Leave them to manage themselves
D. Push them without understanding
